data.title: Publications, methodology // and updates.
data.subtitle: Whether you are a company, investor, public decision-maker, or analyst, these resources help you understand how contribution is measured — and how the framework evolves.

data.title: The complete methodology // in one reference document.
data.subtitle: The White Paper presents the complete architecture of the Climate Contribution Framework — from founding rationale to development prospects.

data.title: From reduction to contribution
data.subtitle: The rationale behind the CCF: why measuring only carbon footprint is insufficient to account for real corporate climate action.

data.title: The three-pillar architecture
data.subtitle: Complete description of pillars A (Reduction), B (Solutions), and C (Finance), their scope, sub-pillars, and source frameworks mobilized.

data.title: The scoring system and sector weighting
data.subtitle: How the Translation Kit, credibility coefficients, and sector coefficients α, β, γ work — with numerical examples.

data.title: The three key results
data.subtitle: Contribution potential, actual contribution, and contribution performance: how they are calculated, how to read them, and how to compare them.

data.title: Framework limitations
data.subtitle: Excluded scopes, sector calibration assumptions, and methodological vigilance points documented by the authors.

data.title: Future developments
data.subtitle: Planned evolution axes: sector extension, integration of new frameworks, improved score granularity, and framework governance.

data.title: Frequently asked questions.
data.subtitle: The most frequently asked questions about the Climate Contribution Framework — its scope, how it works, and its relationships with existing frameworks.

data.title: What does the CCF measure?
data.subtitle: A multidimensional framework that assesses emissions reductions in the value chain, avoided emissions through products and services, climate finance, and influence on public policies. It captures direct and indirect impacts to reflect the full spectrum of climate action.

data.title: How does the CCF ensure fair comparison across sectors?
data.subtitle: The CCF applies context-adjusted weighting according to business sector, geography, company size, and profit margins. High-impact sectors are assessed on broader contribution ranges. This approach enables peer comparisons while reflecting structurally different roles in the transition.

data.title: What is the relationship between the CCF and existing frameworks?
data.subtitle: The CCF is designed to complement — not replace — frameworks like SBTi or the GHG Protocol. It fills current gaps by integrating indirect impacts and climate finance, aligning with best practice methodologies, and providing a unified benchmark covering multiple frameworks.

data.title: Who develops the CCF?
data.subtitle: The initiative is led by Sweep and the Mirova Research Center. Methodological development is carried out with I Care by BearingPoint and Winrock International. A group of sponsoring companies supports the project, and an independent advisory committee composed of experts from leading climate organizations provides oversight.

data.title: What is the relationship between the CCF and the Sweep platform?
data.subtitle: The CCF defines the contribution assessment methodology. Sweep provides the technology to operationalize it: data collection and consolidation, methodology application, calculation of the three indicators, scenario simulation, and action planning. The framework sets the rules, Sweep provides the digital engine.

data.title: What is the long-term ambition of the CCF?
data.subtitle: To establish the CCF as the global reference for corporate climate contribution, by integrating and building on existing frameworks. Ultimately, the goal is to influence public policies so that every company is required to define and publish a Contribution Plan.

data.title: Is the CCF publicly accessible?
data.subtitle: Yes. The CCF is designed to be accessible to all — its development and dissemination being funded by philanthropy. The White Paper, methodological documentation, and updates are freely available.

data.title: Want to understand // how your company // could be assessed?
data.subtitle: Start the 3-minute benchmark or download the White Paper to explore the methodology in detail.
